ground_truth.title: 7 days of healthy meals on a budget
ground_truth.author: Tracy Parker, Dietitian
ground_truth.main_content: If you're trying to keep food costs down, planning your meals in advance can help you make healthier choices, save money and reduce waste. This article provides a week of dietitian-approved meals for around £29 for 1 person, around £58 for 2 people, or around £116 for 4 people. This is just under the average weekly cost of nearly £31 per person for food and non-alcoholic drinks in the UK. The meal plan includes 7 days of affordable breakfasts, lunches, dinners and snacks, as well as a comprehensive shopping list with all the ingredients needed. Each day includes the recommended 5 portions of fruit and vegetables, as well as more than the daily fibre target of 30g. The plan provides around 1,500 calories per day. The full plan meets the UK Government's Eatwell Guide's recommended proportions of fruit and vegetables, starchy carbohydrates, protein, dairy, and healthy fats. Daily meal plans with recipes include: Monday - Porridge, spicy carrot and lentil soup with bread, jacket potato with sardines; Tuesday - Muesli, leftover soup, marry me chicken with potatoes and beans; Wednesday - Boiled egg on toast, jacket potato with beans, beetroot barley risotto; Thursday - Banana on toast, egg sandwich, wholewheat spaghetti with sardines; Friday - Muesli with banana, cheese sandwich, mushroom and cauliflower frittata; Saturday - Porridge, Indian-spiced scrambled eggs, tuna and sweetcorn pasta bake; Sunday - Poached egg with mushrooms, chicken and vegetable traybake, baked citrus pears with custard. Approximate daily costs range from £2.70 to £6.81.
with: Want to eat a balanced diet without breaking the bank? Try this 7-day meal plan created by BHF Senior Dietitian Tracy Parker.
In this article, we've planned a week of dietitian-approved meals for around £29 for 1 person, around £58 for 2 people, around £116 for 4 people.
Each day includes the recommended 5 portions of fruit and vegetables, as well as more than the daily fibre target of 30g.
This meal plan provides around 1,500 calories per day, which may be appropriate for some people, particularly those aiming to lose weight.
The full plan meets the UK Government's Eatwell Guide's recommended proportions of fruit and vegetables, starchy carbohydrates, protein, dairy, and healthy fats.
